rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY PARCELL et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS B et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesay, March 4, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for Summary Judgment denied. Triable issue of fact exists whether decedent was exposed to defendant's products and causation. Objection to Hall declaration overruled. Objection to Exhibit C and Exhibits 1 & 2 attached to Ay declaration sustained. Objection to Ay declaration sustained. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R TOZZI et al VS. WALTER E. MCGUIRE REAL ESTATE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesay, March 4, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT HENRY SHINEFIELD'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. No facts alleged specific to Defendant Shinefield regarding his role in conspiring to defraud plaintiffs while plaintiffs deciding to purchase the property. Plaintffs have not alleged that Defendant Shinefield did anything prior to plaintiffs accepting the counter offer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/VC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R TOZZI et al VS. WALTER E. MCGUIRE REAL ESTATE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder In Demuurer By Deft Henry R. Shinefield, M.D. To Pltfs' Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT GIAN MARTINELLI'S Notice Of Joinder In Demuurer By Defendant Henry R. Shinefield, M.D. To Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint. Appearances:Alex M. Weyand, of Peterson Weyand Martin, (415) 399-2900, appearing for Defendant, Gian Martinelli, and moving party; John Carey, of Wild Carey & Fife, (415) 837-3101, appearing for Defendant Shinefield; and moving party; John A. Loveman, Esq., and Max H. Stern, of Duane Morris, (415) 957-3129, appearing for Plaintiffs; and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. No facts alleged specific to Defendant Shinefield regarding his role in conspiring to defraud plaintiffs while plaintiffs deciding to purchase the property. Plaintffs have not alleged that Defendant Shinefield did anything prior to plaintiffs accepting the counter offer Counsel stipulate to 20 days to answer/respond, once amended complaint is filed. Counsel also waive notice to the 10 days, and 20 days, respectively. (302/CWW/VC) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

rulings.Case Title: TAROUB H RUSNAK VS. JANICE SALAMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesay, March 4, 2009, line 12, PLAINTIFF TAROUB RUSNAK Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees. Motion for attorneys' fees is granted. Plaintiff is awarded $470,082.25 attorneys' fees. Plus interest at the legal rate of ten per cent from the date of the entry of judgment. The Court finds the hours incurred and the rates charged were reasonable, and the Lodestar amount is reasonable and appropriate. =(302/CWW)

The Court finds the hours incurred and the rates charged were reasonable, and the Lodestar amount is reasonable and appropriate. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SAYED BASHIR RAHIMI VS. TAXI COMMISSION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Sayed Rahimi'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling Of His Ex Parte Application For Order For Stay License And Card Suspension Pending Final Determination Of Writ Proceeding Filed Jan-30-2009 In Pro Per
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesay, March 4, 2009, line 14, Petitioner Sayed Rahimi's motion for reconsideration of ruling of his ex parte application for order for stay license and Card suspension pending final determination of Writ. Peremptory Challenge to Judge Woolard pursuant to CCP section 170.6 denied as untimely. Motion for reconsideration denied. Petitioner has not alleged any new law or facts that were not available at the time the original or second applications were made. =(302/CWW)
